other_parties.name: Conyers Trust Company (Cayman) Ltd as trustee for the IFM Global Infrastructure Fund

merger_description:
AustralianSuper Pty Ltd as trustee for AustralianSuper (**AustralianSuper**) proposes to acquire additional interests in the IFM Global Infrastructure (Australia) Trust (**IFM GIAT**), a feeder fund to the IFM Global Infrastructure Fund (**GIF Master Fund**) (the **Acquisition**).

AustralianSuper is an Australian industry superannuation and pension fund. It offers its members services relating to superannuation, investment, retirement, and insurance. It has over 3.6 million members and manages over $410 billion in funds (as at 31 December 2025).

The IFM GIAT is a fund established for the purpose of investing in the GIF Master Fund. The GIF Master Fund is a global infrastructure fund that invests in long-duration, illiquid core infrastructure assets across transport, utilities, energy transmission and digital infrastructure.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
AustralianSuper Pty Ltd as trustee for AustralianSuper
(AustralianSuper) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of additional interests in the IFM Global
Infrastructure (Australia) Trust (IFM GIAT), a feeder fund to the IFM
Global Infrastructure Fund (GIF Master Fund), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, AustralianSuper, is an Australian industry
superannuation and pension fund. It offers its members services
relating to superannuation, investment, retirement, and insurance. It
has over 3.6 million members and manages over $410 billion in
funds (as at 31 December 2025).
The target, IFM GIAT, is a fund established for the purpose of
investing in the GIF Master Fund. The GIF Master Fund is a global
infrastructure fund that invests in long-duration, illiquid core
infrastructure assets across transport, utilities, energy transmission
and digital infrastructure.
Both AustralianSuper and GIF Master Fund have interests in Sydney
Airport. AustralianSuper also has an interest in Perth Airport.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. the increment to AustralianSuper’s existing interest in IFM
GIAT, and the GIF Master Fund, is small
b. while there is common ownership between AustralianSuper
and GIF Master Fund’s investments in Sydney Airport, the
increment to AustralianSuper’s existing interest is very small
c. while there is cross-ownership between AustralianSuper’s
interests in Sydney Airport and Perth Airport, there is limited
competition between airports.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
